method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov/robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted www.nyc.gov/site/taxcommission (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace + mPulse/Boomerang RUM + AWS ALB). Read the appeal process pages and the forms index — the appeal is an 'Application for Correction' filed on PDF forms (TC101 Class 2/4, TC108 Class 1, TC109 condos, TC106 exemptions, income & expense TC201/TC203/TC208/TC214, TC309 accountant certification at $5.4M+) or through the Commission's online filing system; a $175 fee applies at $2M+ assessed value. Queried the Socrata Discovery API: there is NO 'Tax Commission (TC)' agency label — the two relevant assets are published under 'Office of Administrative Tax Appeals (OATA)' and were pulled with column schemas.
headline_findings: There is NO 'Tax Commission (TC)' agency in NYC Open Data; the Tax Commission's two datasets are published under 'Office of Administrative Tax Appeals (OATA)', the umbrella that now houses it.

The open data is thin and OUTCOME-ONLY: Assessment Actions (4nft-bihw, granted reductions/reclassifications) and Open Article 7 Petitions (aht6-vxai, judicial escalation to NY Supreme Court). Neither describes the appeal process itself.

The core transaction — filing an assessment appeal (Application for Correction) — has no machine-readable contract. It lives only in PDF forms (TC101/TC108/TC109/TC106/TC201…) and the Commission's online filing system, with a March 15/16 deadline and a $175 fee at $2M+ assessed value.
The domain sits on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace/mPulse/AWS ALB); the Tax Commission has no distinct platform of its own and no content or filing API.

Relationship: DOF SETS the tentative assessment (Notice of Property Value); the Tax Commission HEARS the appeal. Net-new write surface: file a property-tax assessment appeal (AssessmentAppeal). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 2 datasets under 'Office of Administrative Tax Appeals (OATA)': Assessment Actions (4nft-bihw) and Open Article 7 Petitions (aht6-vxai). SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The only real, open API here — outcome data only.
Application for Correction (TC101/TC108/TC109/TC106 + income & expense TC201/203/208/214 + TC309). No JSON/OpenAPI surface; PDF forms and a browser-only filing flow.
About, how-to-appeal, forms, rules, guidelines, annual reports. No content API.
server-timing ak_p, alt-svc h3 on the informational site.
x-oneagent-js-injection; BOOMR mPulse snippet in page markup.

fruit.name: Assessment Actions (granted reductions / reclassifications)
fruit.notes: The Tax Commission's published actions reducing assessments or reclassifying property. Keyed on Tax Year + Borough Code/Block/Lot + Tax Class Code, with Granted Reduction Amount (total actual AV). Outcomes only — no application-level detail.

fruit.name: Open Article 7 Petitions (judicial escalation)
opendata_match: Open Article 7 Petitions aht6-vxai (10c) — OATA label
fruit.notes: Open petitions challenging assessments in NY State Supreme Court under Article 7 of the RPTL: index number/year, petitioner, attorney (name + identifier), note-of-issue codes. The escalation that follows a Tax Commission determination.

fruit.name: Property / tax lot (subject of appeal)
fruit.notes: The Borough-Block-Lot (BBL) tax lot being appealed. DOF sets its tentative assessed value (Notice of Property Value); no Tax-Commission-owned property API. Property fields appear only inside the Assessment Actions outcome rows.

fruit.name: Representative (attorney / agent)
opendata_match: partial — attorney name/identifier in Open Article 7 Petitions aht6-vxai
fruit.notes: The attorney or agent filing on the owner's behalf. Captured on the application forms; only the litigation attorney surfaces in the Article 7 dataset.

fruit.name: Determination / offer (hearing result)
fruit.notes: The Commission's decision on an application — an offer of reduction, reclassification, or no change. Per-application detail lives only in the online filing system; the aggregate published record is Assessment Actions.

fruit.name: File an assessment appeal (Application for Correction)
fruit.notes: The single most important transaction — challenge DOF's tentative assessed value and/or tax class for a tax year. No API and no Open Data twin; lives only in PDF forms (TC101/TC108/TC109/TC106 + income & expense TC201/203/208/214 + TC309 at $5.4M+) and the online filing system. March 15/16 deadline; $175 fee at $2M+ AV. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' CMS (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace + mPulse/Boomerang + AWS ALB) plus a browser-only online filing system; no distinct Tax Commission platform.
meta.netnew: AssessmentAppeal — file a property-tax assessment appeal (Application for Correction).
meta.tagline: An independent property-tax appeals body whose intake is PDF forms and whose only open data is a thin trail of outcomes.
meta.headline: DOF sets the assessment; the Tax Commission hears the appeal — but that appeal is filed on paper forms with no API, while the two open datasets show only the reductions granted and the court petitions that follow.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis + a browser-only filing system; nothing Tax-Commission-specific.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or filing API — PDF forms and a web-only online filing flow.
scorecard_notes.openData: Two thin, outcome-only datasets, published under the OATA label, not a Tax Commission label.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core process entities (application, determination, property, representative) are absent from open data; only outcomes appear.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API; the appeal is filed by PDF form or online filing system with a $175 fee and a hard deadline.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind.
scorecard_notes.ownership: The Commission does publish two of its own datasets, but on shared infrastructure it does not control.
